2603.18881.title: Geography According to ChatGPT -- How Generative AI Represents and Reasons about Geography
2603.18881.paper_summary: Understanding how AI will represent and reason about geography should be a key concern for all of us, as the broader public increasingly interacts with spaces and places through these systems. Similarly, in line with the nature of foundation models, our own research often relies on pre-trained models. Hence, understanding what world AI systems construct is as important as evaluating their accuracy, including factual recall. To motivate the need for such studies, we provide three illustrative vignettes, i.e., exploratory probes, in the hope that they will spark lively discussions and follow-up work: (1) Do models form strong defaults, and how brittle are model outputs to minute syntactic variations? (2) Can distributional shifts resurface from the composition of individually benign tasks, e.g., when using AI systems to create personas? (3) Do we overlook deeper questions of understanding when solely focusing on the ability of systems to recall facts such as geographic principles?
2603.18881.paper_summary_zh: 理解人工智慧將如何表現和推理地理應該是我們所有人的關鍵關注，因為更廣泛的公眾越來越多地通過這些系統與空間和地點互動。<lb/>同樣，根據基礎模型的特性，我們自己的研究經常依賴於預訓練模型。<lb/>因此，理解人工智慧系統構建的世界與評估其準確性一樣重要，包括事實回憶。<lb/>為了激發對這類研究的需求，我們提供三個說明性的小插曲，即探索性探針，希望它們能引發生動的討論和後續工作：<lb/>(1) 模型是否形成強烈的默認值，模型輸出對微小語法變化的脆弱性如何？<lb/>(2) 當使用人工智慧系統創建角色時，分佈變化是否會從各個無害任務的組合中重新浮現？<lb/>(3) 當僅專注於系統回憶事實（如地理原則）的能力時，我們是否忽視了更深層次的理解問題？
2603.18881.author: Krzysztof Janowicz et.al.
2603.18881.authors: Krzysztof Janowicz, Gengchen Mai, Rui Zhu, Song Gao, Zhangyu Wang, Yingjie Hu, Lauren Bennett
